challenge: Build with DataHub: The Agent Hackathon
goal: Post CAT's generated datasetProperties, schemaMetadata, ownership, and glossaryTerms aspects to a local DataHub GMS through the Rest.li ingestProposal action, then use the same context to justify safe, approval-gated, and blocked agent actions.

live_ingest_contract.aspect_encoding: Each aspect is serialized as aspect.value JSON with contentType application/json.

dry_run_payloads.purpose: Identify the messy business request dataset and expose CAT decision totals as metadata.
Describe the CSV schema and field confidence before the agent acts.
Attach a known CAT demo data owner so missing ownership can be detected instead of invented.
Mark policy terms such as context-before-action and no unverified outreach.

prerequisites: Node 22+ dependencies installed with npm install or npm ci.
A local DataHub GMS instance reachable at DATAHUB_GMS_URL, usually http://localhost:8080.
No cloud credentials are required for the repo evidence path.
Live posting is intentionally blocked unless the operator passes --post.

commands.name: Regenerate the local CAT decision artifacts
commands.expected: generated-agent-output.json, generated-datahub-metadata.json, and generated-agent-context-packet.json are updated.

commands.name: Preview the DataHub metadata payloads
commands.expected: hackathon-assets/datahub-payload-preview.md lists four DataHub Rest.li ingestProposal bodies without contacting GMS.

commands.name: Post to local DataHub only after GMS is running
commands.expected: The bridge posts four UPSERT MetadataChangeProposal bodies to /aspects?action=ingestProposal and leaves generated-datahub-bridge-plan.json as a receipt.

commands.name: Read context before agent action
commands.expected: generated-mcp-context-read.json shows datahub.get_entity, datahub.get_lineage, and cat.get_agent_context_packet before the approval decision.

commands.name: Reproduce the judge evidence chain
commands.expected: The reproduction receipt confirms payload preview, live runbook, decision trace, readiness, validation, and safety checks.

acceptance_checks.name: aspect coverage
acceptance_checks.detail: 4 DataHub Rest.li ingestProposal payloads are prepared: datasetProperties, schemaMetadata, ownership, glossaryTerms.

acceptance_checks.name: local-first verification
acceptance_checks.detail: Judges can inspect every generated artifact without external credentials or a hosted service.

acceptance_checks.name: live mutation is explicit
acceptance_checks.detail: Only the documented DATAHUB_GMS_URL + --post command mutates a local DataHub instance.

acceptance_checks.name: agent safety remains preserved
acceptance_checks.detail: The blocked external outreach case remains blocked even after the DataHub post path is enabled.

safety_boundary: Do not post to a remote or production DataHub instance from the demo unless the operator intentionally changes DATAHUB_GMS_URL.
Do not add secrets, tokens, or customer data to the generated artifacts.
Do not let the agent send external outreach when contact ownership or approval context is missing.
Treat failed DataHub posting as a setup issue, not permission to bypass context reads.
fallback: If local DataHub is not available during judging, use npm run datahub:payload plus npm run decision:trace. Those commands prove the same aspect payloads and read-before-action decisions in dry-run mode.
